question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch transaction (ATOMIC_BATCH type) where the parentConsensusTimestamp of inner transactions points to a different batch parent, causing onTransaction() to call batchParent.addInnerTransaction() with misattributed inner transactions and reorganize the logical grouping of transactions in the history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordItem where transaction.getBatchKey() is non-null and nonce == 0, but parentConsensusTimestamp does not match any existing batch parent in the context, triggering Utility.handleRecoverableError() which may only log a warning and continue, silently dropping the inner transaction from the batch and altering the transaction history structure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user craft two Entity updates with the same timestampLower value (isSameTimestampLower == true), causing mergeEntity() to copy fields from current to previous in the same-timestamp branch, potentially overwriting the correct entity state with attacker-controlled values and misrepresenting the entity's history at that timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition where previous.hasHistory() is false and current.hasHistory() is true in mergeEntity(), causing dest.setTimestampRange(src.getTimestampRange()) to assign the wrong timestamp range to the entity record, misrepresenting when an entity state change occurred in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeNft()] Can an unprivileged external user craft two NFT updates with the same timestampLower, causing mergeNft() to enter the same-timestamp branch and set cachedNft.setAccountId(newNft.getAccountId()), effectively overwriting the NFT ownership record at that timestamp with an attacker-chosen account, reorganizing the NFT transfer history without a direct fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeNft()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AbstractNft.shouldKeepSpender() logic in mergeNft() to preserve a stale spender/delegatingSpender from a previous NFT state, causing the historical record to show an incorrect approval relationship at a given timestamp and misrepresenting the NFT's authorization history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeToken()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Token update where current.hasHistory() is false, causing mergeToken() to call previous.setTotalSupply(current.getTotalSupply()) and return previous, silently updating the total supply in the current record without creating a history entry, making the supply change invisible in the token's historical timeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeTokenAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TokenAccount update where lastTokenAccount.getTimestampRange().equals(newTokenAccount.getTimestampRange()), triggering the duplicate detection branch that logs a warning and returns lastTokenAccount, causing a legitimate token account state change to be silently dropped and the account's association history to be misrepresented?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onStakingRewardTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StakingRewardTransfer with a manipulated consensusTimestamp that causes Utility.getEpochDay(consensusTimestamp) - 1 to produce a stakePeriodStart in the past, and since the condition checks current.getStakePeriodStart() < stakePeriodStart, cause the entity's stake period start to be set to an incorrect historical value, reorganizing the staking reward history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onTokenAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TokenAirdrop claim transaction where isTokenAccountAlreadyAssociated() returns false due to a race condition between the context check and the tokenAccountRepository.findById() call, causing a duplicate token account association to be persisted and the association history to show two conflicting entries at the same timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeTokenAirdrop()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TokenAirdrop cancel/claim transaction where current.getAmount() is null, causing mergeTokenAirdrop() to copy previous.getAmount() into current, and then set previous.setTimestampUpper(current.getTimestampLower()), potentially closing the previous airdrop record at an incorrect timestamp and misrepresenting the airdrop lifecycle in the history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeSchedule()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Schedule update that sets executedTimestamp to a value earlier than the schedule's creation timestamp, causing mergeSchedule() to persist an executedTimestamp that precedes the schedule's existence in the history, reorganizing the apparent execution order of scheduled transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Topic update where current.getTimestampLower() is earlier than previous.getTimestampLower(), causing previous.setTimestampUpper(current.getTimestampLower()) to set an upper bound that is before the lower bound of the previous record, creating an invalid timestamp range in the topic history that misrepresents when topic configuration changes occurred?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeHook()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Hook update where previous.getDeleted() is true and previous.getTimestampLower().equals(current.getTimestampLower()), causing the adjustment of +1 in mergeHook() to set an incorrect timestampUpper, misrepresenting the deletion and re-creation sequence of the hook in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HookStorage update where previous.isDeleted() is true and current.isDeleted() is false, causing mergeHookStorage() to set previous.setCreatedTimestamp(~current.getCreatedTimestamp() + 1) (a negative timestamp), which when used in a SQL COALESCE upsert could cause the hook storage's creation timestamp to be misrepresented in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Ledger update that always overrides the previous ledger's consensusTimestamp and nodeContributions (since mergeLedger() always copies current to previous), causing the ledger history to reflect attacker-controlled node contribution data at a given consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onContractStateChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractStateChange where valueWritten is null but isMigration() is true, causing onContractStateChange() to use valueRead as the contract state value and persist it as a state write, misrepresenting the contract's storage history by recording a read as a write at that consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: mergeContractState()] Can an unprivileged external user craft two ContractStateChange events for the same slot with different consensusTimestamps, causing mergeContractState() to update previous.setValue() and previous.setModifiedTimestamp() without creating a history record, making the intermediate state change invisible in the contract storage history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onNftTransferList()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction containing an NftTransfer with serialNumber == NftTransfer.WILDCARD_SERIAL_NUMBER (treasury change), causing onNftTransferList() to call flushNftState() and nftRepository.updateTreasury() with attacker-controlled receiverAccountId and senderAccountId, reorganizing the NFT treasury ownership history in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: flush()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an exception in batchPersister.persist() for one entity type during flush(), causing the entire transaction to be rolled back via the @Transactional annotation, and then replay a modified version of the same record file (with reordered transactions) that succeeds on retry, effectively replacing the original transaction history with the reordered version?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Retryable annotation on RecordFileParser.parse() — which retries on any exception except OutOfMemoryError — to cause repeated retries with a modified record file (served from an attacker-controlled bucket) that eventually succeeds, replacing the originally-attempted transaction history with a reordered version?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileData with a filename that starts with a path traversal sequence (e.g., '../../../etc/') such that getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() resolves to an unintended directory, causing archived stream files to overwrite legitimate files and corrupt the local record of processed transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user set the hiero.mirror.importer.network property to a value containing a delimiter (e.g., 'mainnet-attacker'), causing getNetwork() to return 'mainnet' while getNetworkPrefix() returns 'attacker', and exploit this split to route the importer to an attacker-controlled bucket prefix while appearing to use the mainnet network configuration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network to a value that causes HederaNetwork.hasCutover() to return false, disabling the block stream cutover logic and forcing the importer to continue using the legacy record stream path, potentially causing it to ingest an older or attacker-controlled stream that reorganizes the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network to 'demo' to trigger HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess() returning true, enabling anonymous access to the configured bucket, and then upload attacker-crafted record files to the demo bucket that contain a reordered transaction history which the importer will ingest without credential checks?
